A year ago, Dalian Yingbo FC were number 9 in the table with 13 points. Now they are number 3 with 18 points.

ai_preview.text:
**Both teams are limping into round 13**, but someone's gotta get three points — and it probably won't be pretty. 🔴

Shenzhen Peng City can't buy a win lately (1W in last five), while **Dalian Yingbo FC** are somehow worse, coughing up goals at an alarming rate. The visitors have shipped 9 in their last three matches alone. That's the kind of defensive vulnerability Shenzhen's attack can exploit, even on a bad day.

Key storylines:

- **Wesley Moraes** and **D. O. Sekyere** leading Shenzhen's charge against a Dalian defense that's been genuinely porous
- **Dalian's** road form is abysmal — they've lost 2-3, 1-3, and 0-3 on their travels, so expect them to struggle against even middling opposition
- The **H2H** slightly favours the visitors historically, but form says ignore that entirely

Expected lineups:

- **Shenzhen Peng City**: P. Peng, R. Hu, Y. Yang, Z. Jiang, F. Benković, Wesley Moraes, E. Karzev, D. O. Sekyere
- **Dalian Yingbo FC**: Z. Huang, M. Traoré, L. Zhuoyi, J. Bi, F. Acheampong, I. Alimi, C. Malele, Y. Mingrui

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 1.5**: Over 1.5 goals

Prediction: **2-1 Shenzhen** — coin flip on the scoreline, but there's enough offensive juice and defensive chaos here to expect multiple goals. 📊

funfacts.sentence: Qingdao West Coast FC haven't won in their last 8 games.
Qingdao West Coast FC haven't won any of their last 3 games against Beijing Guoan.
Qingdao West Coast FC's home record this season: 1-4-0.

ai_preview.text:
**Qingdao's draw machine meets Beijing's firepower** — West Coast are basically allergic to losses lately, but Guoan have come to play in the Super League. 🔥

Qingdao are unbeaten in five (1W 4D), riding the vibe of a road win at Dalian, though three straight draws at home suggest they might sit deep. Beijing, meanwhile, just torched Dalian 3-0 and have won two of their last five, looking sharp on the road.

Key storylines:

- **Beijing Guoan's attack vs. Qingdao's back line** — Guoan's recent firepower (3-0, 4-2 wins) will test a Qingdao defense that's been sturdy but passive
- **Qingdao's patient build** — Without Riccieli, West Coast lean on Rezende and Davidson to control tempo and frustrate visiting attacks
- **Head-to-head history** — Guoan have dominated the last four meetings (3 wins, 1 draw), a huge mental edge

Expected lineups:

- **Qingdao West Coast**: H. Li, S. Memisevic, G. Wang, Y. Dong, P. Wang, Rezende, Davidson, N. Luz, Aziz, J. Weiwei, A. Aisikaer
- **Beijing Guoan**: H. Sen, G. Ramos, B. Yang, G. Wang, A. Abduhamit, A. Konte, S. Erjini'ao, Z. Xizhe, L. Liangming, Z. Yuning, F. Abreu

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 1.5**: Over 1.5 goals

Prediction:

**1-1 draw** — Qingdao's draw streak stays alive, but Beijing grab a crucial away point.

funfacts.sentence: Shanghai Port haven't won any of their last 3 games against Chengdu Rongcheng.
When playing at home, Chengdu Rongcheng have not lost to Shanghai Port in their last 3 encounters.
Chengdu Rongcheng's home record this season: 6-0-0.

ai_preview.text:
**Chengdu's on an absolute tear** — five straight wins at home, and Shanghai Port can't seem to string together anything resembling form. This looks like a classic heavyweight-in-crisis vs. the red-hot underdog showdown. 🔥

Rongcheng have been ruthless (scored in every recent match, conceded just one), while Port are stuttering through draws and losses. The head-to-head tells the whole story: Chengdu dominate this fixture historically.

Key storylines:

- **Chengdu Rongcheng's home fortress** keeps tightening — five wins, solid defensive shape, and the midfield tandem of W. Silva and W. Shihao controlling tempo
- **Shanghai Port's misfiring attack** relies on L. Yongtao and P. Ampem to spark something, but they've lacked conviction in recent weeks
- **Home-field edge is real** — Shuangliu Sports Centre has been Chengdu's fortress this season

Expected lineups:

- **Chengdu Rongcheng**: L. Dianzuo, E. Sorokin, H. Hetao, H. Pengfei, W. Silva, W. Shihao, Rômulo, F. Silva, B. Abuduwaili
- **Shanghai Port**: Y. Junling, X. Y. Alex, Z. Wei, U. Yusup, B. Shimeng, M. Vital, L. Zhurun, P. Ampem, L. Yongtao

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 1.5**: Over 1.5 goals

Prediction:

**Chengdu Rongcheng 2–0 Shanghai Port**. Rongcheng have the edge in form, head-to-head record, and home momentum. Port lack firepower right now.
